stories.headline: Dow closes above 51,000, S&P 500 logs ninth straight weekly gain as AI rally + Iran ceasefire optimism cap record May
stories.summary: Friday May 29 capped a strong month: Dow Industrials +0.72% to 51,032.46, S&P 500 +0.22% to 7,580.06 (ninth consecutive weekly gain, longest streak since 2023), and Nasdaq Composite +0.26% to 26,972.62. For the month, Nasdaq jumped roughly 8%, S&P 500 about 5%, and Dow about 3%, driven by Dell's AI-server earnings and growing optimism on the 60-day US-Iran ceasefire framework.

perspectives.keyFraming: Records cap the strongest tech month since the post-pandemic rally, with AI demand and the Iran framework driving simultaneous risk-on bid.
perspectives.summary: Reuters/Yahoo Finance and TheStreet lead the Friday close: Dow above 51,000, S&P 500 7,580.06, Nasdaq 26,972.62; ninth weekly gain for the S&P is the longest streak since 2023. Drivers were Dell's earnings beat (stock surged up to 40%) plus White House signals it would soon endorse the Iran framework. Treasury yields drifted lower (10-year ~4.45%), supportive of multiples.

sources.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq cap winning month with fresh records in AI-fueled rally as Trump nears Iran decision
Stock Market Today (May 29, 2026): S&P 500 good for ninth consecutive week of gains

stories.headline: Bitcoin slides to ~$73k as a record spot-ETF outflow streak diverges from the AI-driven stock rally
stories.summary: BTC fell to around $73,300 — roughly -4.6% on the week — with ETH near $1,990 and SOL near $82, pressured by a record ~10-session, ~$3 billion outflow streak from US spot bitcoin ETFs and firmer oil on stalled Iran talks. The CFTC has authorized fully regulated US perpetual futures, anchoring the dominant global derivatives format domestically. Crypto's weakness stands in sharp contrast to equities setting records on the AI trade.

perspectives.keyFraming: Crypto's late-May correction is institutional, not retail — and US regulators are simultaneously expanding the regulated rail.
perspectives.summary: CoinDesk reporting notes BTC's two-month winning streak is in jeopardy after the ETF exodus, though cumulative US spot BTC inflows remain near $58.7B. The CFTC's perpetual-futures green light meaningfully changes US market structure by anchoring the dominant global derivatives format domestically.

sources.title: Bitcoin price news: BTC off the lows on Trump post, but set to close month of May with losses
Bitcoin, ether, solana slide as AI trade continues to rip higher

stories.headline: Brent ticks to ~$93, WTI to ~$90 in Sunday electronic trade as Iran-deal uncertainty and Lebanon offensive lift war premium
stories.summary: OilPrice.com spot showed Brent near $93.37 (+2.47%) and WTI near $89.85 (+2.85%) in Sunday electronic trade, both well above Friday's New York close of $92.56 and $87.20. The bid reflects uncertainty over Trump's withheld Iran-ceasefire sign-off and Israel's deepening Lebanon operation, with seven OPEC+ producers due to meet June 7 to set July output. Wholesale RBOB gasoline rose ~2.5% to $3.11/gal; heating oil and natural gas each up about 2.4–2.9%.

perspectives.keyFraming: Electronic-trade framing: weekend bid on Brent/WTI says the market is hedging against an Iran-deal collapse and a wider Lebanon war.
perspectives.summary: OilPrice.com Sunday spot quotes show Brent and WTI both rebounding from Friday's close — Brent +$2.06 to $93.26, WTI +$2.27 to $89.72 — with downstream products and natural gas moving in tandem. The bid coincides with Trump's unresolved Iran framework and IDF operations beyond the Litani; both raise the probability that the May war premium does not unwind.

stories.headline: Oil jumps and US stocks slip after Iran halts US talks and threatens to fully close the Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: WTI rose more than 5% toward the low-$90s and Brent climbed near $95 on June 1 after Iranian state media said Tehran halted mediated messaging with Washington over Israel's Lebanon operations and threatened to fully block the Strait of Hormuz. US equity futures turned lower, pulling indices back from May's record run, and traders revived bets that energy-driven inflation could force the Fed to hold or even hike.

perspectives.keyFraming: Supply-shock mechanics: a renewed Hormuz threat puts roughly a fifth of seaborne crude at risk, reviving inflation and rate worries.
perspectives.summary: CNBC and Yahoo Finance report US crude jumped more than 6% intraday on the report that Iran would halt talks and move to close Hormuz, with Brent up around 5%. Coverage frames geopolitics as overriding May's record equity rally; traders re-priced Fed expectations as the conflict's energy-price channel reasserted itself, with safe-haven gold and the dollar firming.

sources.title: U.S. oil jumps more than 6% on report Iran will halt talks with U.S. and completely block Hormuz
Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq waver as oil surges
U.S. oil jumps more than 7% on report Iran will halt talks with U.S. and completely block Hormuz

perspectives.keyFraming: Markets stay resilient — tech strength from Computex cushions the energy hit, and the administration signals the Iran deal may still hold.
perspectives.summary: TheStreet's market blog notes that after May's record-setting run, US futures wavered rather than tumbled, with Computex-driven tech optimism offsetting energy and small-cap weakness. The lean-right read emphasizes Trump's confidence that the situation 'will all work out' and treats the oil spike as a geopolitical premium rather than a fundamental shift in the disinflation trajectory.

sources.title: Stock Market Today (June 1, 2026): Nasdaq Futures Rise After May's Record-Setting Run

stories.headline: Bitcoin slips below ~$74,000 and Ether tests $2,000 as crypto extends a multi-week decline into June
stories.summary: Bitcoin traded below about $74,000, Ether near the $2,000 psychological level and Solana around $81 on June 1 as the oil-and-inflation risk-off and rising rate-hike odds pressured risk assets. The slide extends a roughly three-week decline and follows record US spot-Bitcoin ETF outflows, even as the CFTC's recent green light for perpetual futures expands the regulated US crypto rail.

perspectives.keyFraming: Crypto is trading as a high-beta risk asset, falling alongside equities as the inflation and rate-hike scare deepens.
perspectives.summary: FXStreet's Asian-session wrap reports the three-week decline continued, with Ether's drop toward $2,000 triggering loss realization and Bitcoin and Solana also lower. Analysts frame the move as macro-driven — the oil shock and revived Fed-hike odds sapping appetite for risk — rather than a crypto-specific catalyst.

sources.title: Cryptocurrencies Price Prediction: Solana, Bitcoin & Ethereum – Asian Wrap 01 June

perspectives.keyFraming: Crypto-native commentary fixates on whether $2,000 ETH and sub-$74K BTC mark capitulation or the start of a deeper leg down.
perspectives.summary: Crypto-native outlets debate whether key support levels will hold, parsing on-chain flows and ETF outflows for signs of a bottom. The community framing centers on positioning and sentiment — liquidations, leverage and the psychological weight of round numbers — against the backdrop of the broader macro risk-off.

stories.headline: US stocks split: Nvidia-led AI rally lifts Nasdaq while the oil surge weighs on the Dow and cyclicals
stories.summary: US stocks diverged Monday as oil's spike on the collapse of US-Iran talks hit cyclicals while AI names rallied. The Nasdaq edged higher and the S&P 500 was roughly flat-to-up, led by Nvidia after its new PC chip launch, while the Dow slipped toward ~50,800 and the Russell 2000 fell. The split followed the S&P's ninth straight up week and a roughly 5% May gain.

perspectives.keyFraming: A two-speed tape: AI optimism versus an oil-driven inflation scare split the indices intraday.
perspectives.summary: CNBC reported the S&P rose to start June 'but it's all tech' as the oil spike hit the rest of the market, with Nvidia leading and energy-sensitive names lagging. Yahoo Finance noted the Dow, S&P and Nasdaq wavering as crude surged, framing the day as rotation rather than direction.

sources.title: S&P 500 rises to start June, but it's all tech as oil spike hits rest of the market: Live updates
Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq waver as oil surges

perspectives.keyFraming: Markets-focused read: oil offset the Nvidia catalyst, leaving the Dow flat as energy weighed on value and cyclicals.
perspectives.summary: The Motley Fool reported markets roughly flat as the oil spike offset the new Nvidia chip, with energy fears blunting the AI-led upside across the broader tape.

sources.title: Stock Market Today, June 1: Markets Flat as Oil Spike Offsets New Nvidia Chip

stories.headline: Markets price near-certain June Fed hold as the oil shock revives inflation fears; Warsh set to chair
stories.summary: Markets priced overwhelming odds the Fed holds its 3.50-3.75% range at the June 16-17 meeting as the Middle East oil shock revived inflation fears, with traders also seeing a real chance of no cuts in all of 2026. Headline CPI ran about 3.8% year over year in April. Powell's term ended May 15; Kevin Warsh is expected to chair the meeting, which includes updated economic projections.

perspectives.keyFraming: An energy-price shock has effectively taken June cuts off the table and dented the full-year easing case.
perspectives.summary: Morningstar reported the odds of a 2026 rate cut fading as the oil-driven inflation shock keeps the Fed cautious into a leadership handover, with markets pricing a near-certain June hold.

sources.title: As Powell Closes Out Term as Fed Chair, Odds of Rate Cut in 2026 Vanish

perspectives.keyFraming: Live betting markets quantify the hold conviction in real time.
perspectives.summary: Polymarket's June Fed event showed traders pricing an overwhelming probability of no change at the June meeting, consistent with the post-oil-shock hawkish repricing.

sources.title: Fed Decision in June? Trading Odds & Predictions 2026
